message:
chore(beads): close wa-nu4.3.9.18

Sync close transition for version-smoke CI job landed in 176d89b6.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -37,6 +37,8 @@
 {"id":"ft-48xy0","title":"storage.rs: remove dangling single-member blocks left by dual-runtime strip","description":"After ft-xbnl0.2.5 (9e1bd63b) stripped all #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")] guards, the wrappers survived as empty curly-brace blocks around single return statements. Examples:

crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:10543-10548
    {
        let cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);
        return self.create_reservation_with_cx(&cx, ...).await;
    }
crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:10588-10591 (release_reservation)
crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs: ~60+ similar occurrences across all cx-routed entry points.

Each site has the form:
    pub async fn foo(...) -> Result<T> {
        {
            let cx = ...;
            return self.foo_with_cx(...).await;
        }
    }

Not a bug. Cosmetic cleanup. Makes storage.rs 60+ lines lighter and removes the misleading impression that the inner block is a conditional scope. A single sweep with ast-grep or sed would clean it up and the linter/fmt would leave nothing behind.

Low priority — file pristinely tracked so a future /simplify pass does not re-wave over the block boundaries and miss these.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:16:46.113101Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:24.258101Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:24.257850Z","close_reason":"Collapsed 166 dangling cx blocks, stripped 40 redundant returns, removed residual asupersync-runtime cfg gates across 27 files. -504 net lines. cargo check clean. Commit fdd1e817.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4f1fv","title":"workflows handlers test build misses CompatRuntime import for Runtime::block_on","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:03.547663Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:14.479058Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:14.478891Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4o5mb","title":"[conformance] wa.search / wa.get_text / wa.send / wa.wait_for lack schema+envelope golden roundtrip","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance.rs` pins envelope + schema conformance for exactly ONE tool: `wa.rules_list` (tests at lines 237-294, 313-346). `conformance_mcp_manifest.rs` pins the MANIFEST (tool schemas, additionalProperties=false, resource templates) but does NOT exercise the per-tool request → response envelope roundtrip.

`grep -oE '"wa\.[a-z_]+"' crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance.rs | sort -u` → only `"wa.rules_list"`.

Out of 29 advertised `wa.*` tools in `tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`, 28 have zero call-and-assert-envelope coverage in the conformance suite. The four core operator-facing tools are:

* `wa.search` — JSON envelope shape, hit schema, TOON parity, pane_filter/since_filter/until_filter/mode/metrics fields
* `wa.get_text` — pane_text map shape (ok/error variants), truncated + truncation_info edges
* `wa.send` — sender/response envelope (distinct from get_text), error codes for denied sends
* `wa.wait_for` — matched/elapsed_ms/polls/is_regex fields, timeout response envelope, rule_id match path

## Shape of fix

Mirror the `mcp_conformance_rules_list_*` test pattern (lines 237-294 in mcp_conformance.rs):

1. spawn_client() → call_tool("wa.search", …)
2. parse_tool_envelope() → assert_success_envelope_shape() → assert_*_data_matches_documented_schema()
3. Also pin: invalid-format rejection, invalid-args hint text, TOON parity where applicable.

Existing helpers (`assert_object_matches_documented_schema`, `assert_common_envelope_fields`, `assert_success_envelope_shape`, `assert_invalid_args_envelope_shape`) already generalise — the new tests should reuse them verbatim.

## Why this matters

The MCP manifest conformance is the FREEZE on the advertised schema. Without per-tool envelope tests, a refactor can pass `conformance_mcp_manifest` (schema still looks right) while silently returning a non-conforming response body — e.g. field renamed in `ScannerHit` → clients break but tests stay green. ft-bs0ec froze this for `agent_inventory`; the four core operator tools deserve the same treatment.

## Scope guard

One new test file `mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs` (or additions to `mcp_conformance.rs`) with 4× (success + invalid-args) = 8 tests. ~250 LOC. No production changes.

## Explicitly out of scope

Mission / tx / events / cass families (separate bead if needed). Manifest tests (already covered). Resource templates (already covered).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:57.026144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:46:38.216319Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3608,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb","author":"jemanuel","text":"Blocker (pane3, 2026-04-23): fastmcp-rust enforces tool input_schema type validation at the JSON-RPC boundary — wrong-typed required fields (e.g. pane_id string where integer required) return McpError::InvalidParams from call_tool BEFORE reaching the per-tool handler's FT-MCP-0001 envelope path. The pattern mcp_conformance.rs:280 uses for wa.rules_list (optional string agent_type) cannot be replayed for wa.search/get_text/send/wait_for via wrong-typed-required inputs. Next-hop approach: pin the SUCCESS envelope shape via {timeout_secs:1, pane_id:999, pattern:'never-matches', format:'json'} for wait_for (times out in ~1s, returns Ok envelope with matched:false/elapsed_ms/polls). Draft tests left UNTRACKED at crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs — first pass covers invalid-args envelopes and doesn't work as noted. Bead stays in_progress for handoff.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:27:29Z"}]}

Cover success and invalid-args envelopes without editing mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:32.760784Z","created_by":"LilacBay","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:32.760784Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.1","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:32.760784Z","created_by":"LilacBay","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-4o5mb.2","title":"[conformance] wa.events lacks schema+envelope golden roundtrip","description":"Create a dedicated MCP conformance test file for wa.events using the golden-capture helper pattern already used in mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs. Cover one seeded-success envelope with stored event + annotations plus one invalid-args envelope via out-of-range limit. Reuse manifest schema freeze; do not touch mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs (pane 2 owns it).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:31:02.808965Z","created_by":"LilacBay","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:31:02.808965Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb.2","depends_on_id":"ft-4o5mb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:31:02.808965Z","created_by":"LilacBay","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-4qom2","title":"[SEV] tmux cmd_queue cap only enforced at pop site; unbounded growth when state != Idle","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue has CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000 enforced only inside send_next_command at line 289 via cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286-288 when state != Idle, so the drain-oldest logic never runs if tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse.

Four push sites append to cmd_queue with NO cap check:
- line 196: Event::LayoutChange -> ListAllPanes
- line 223: Event::SessionChanged -> ListCommands
- line 234: Event::WindowAdd -> ListAllWindows
- line 375: split_pane (user split) -> SplitPane

Concrete interleaving: (1) tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse; (2) tmux server is slow/hung (real-world scenario: remote tmux on lagging SSH link, tmux server overloaded, Event::Guarded response delayed); (3) GUI user resizes window repeatedly -> stream of Event::LayoutChange -> each push_back at line 196 runs without cap check; (4) send_next_command early-returns because state != Idle. Queue grows unbounded -> memory leak, eventual OOM.

The CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH comment at line 32-33 explicitly advertises 'prevent unbounded memory growth during event storms' as a safety invariant. That invariant is currently only upheld at the pop site, where it can't run under the exact condition (stuck tmux) that event storms exploit.

Fix: extract the cap-enforcement logic into a push_command_capped helper that applies the drain-oldest + warn/error logging at every push site. The helper runs under the cmd_queue lock (just acquired by the caller), so it's atomic with the push and has no additional locking cost.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:17:26.601099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.160715Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.158111Z","close_reason":"Completed: added push_command_capped() helper and routed all 4 production push sites (LayoutChange/SessionChanged/WindowAdd/split_pane) through it. The hard cap is now enforced at push time, not only at pop time — closing the unbounded-growth window when tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse. cargo check passes clean. Commit 1055cffc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4z7vh","title":"RobotCommands::Workflow builds PolicyEngine without require_prompt_active/command_gate/rules — bypasses operator safety config","description":"AUTH BYPASS (robot mode mutations, Severity: Medium).

crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs has 10 PolicyEngine::new call sites. 8 of them wire require_prompt_active from config + .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()). Two do NOT, both under RobotCommands::Workflow:

Site 1 (main.rs:19831, RobotCommands::Workflow::Run):

  let policy_engine = PolicyEngine::new(
      config.safety.rate_limit_per_pane,
      config.safety.rate_limit_global,
      false, // Don't require prompt active for robot mode
  )
  .with_tuning(&config.tuning);

No command_gate, no policy_rules, and require_prompt_active hardcoded false. This engine is fed into CxPolicyInjector → WorkflowRunner; any workflow step that calls send_text (e.g., handle_compaction sending '/compact', handle_usage_limit sending '/refresh') bypasses:
  - the operator's safety.require_prompt_active=true policy,
  - every [[safety.rules]] custom gate,
  - the [safety.command_gate] allow/deny list.

Site 2 (main.rs:20511, RobotCommands::Workflow::Abort): identical construction. abort_execution_with_cx runs compensation steps through the same injector; any send_text in a compensation path bypasses the same three surfaces.

Compare the correctly-gated paths:
  - RobotCommands::Send at main.rs:17652 uses config.safety.require_prompt_active AND .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules.
  - Non-robot workflow invocations at main.rs:21361, 21533, 25051, 27347 all wire the full policy surface.

NOT a duplicate of ft-48ah6 (read-only policy): this bead is specifically about the write/mutation surface (workflow's send_text / command-bearing steps). ft-48ah6 covered the read/search side.

Fix: replace the hardcoded 'false' with config.safety.require_prompt_active at both sites, and chain .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()) before passing the engine to PolicyGatedInjector. Rate-limit args are already correct.

Regression test: a send inside a robot-invoked workflow must be DENIED when config.safety.require_prompt_active=true AND no prompt is active, matching the direct-send gate behavior. Currently it is allowed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:46:59.925741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084786Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084480Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 834b59f2. Both RobotCommands::Workflow::Run (main.rs:19831) and ::Abort (main.rs:20520) now build PolicyEngine with config.safety.require_prompt_active and chain .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules — matching the construction at RobotCommands::Send (main.rs:17652) and non-robot workflow sites (21361/21533/25051/27347). Regression test robot_workflow_policy_engine_denies_send_when_prompt_required_ft_4z7vh asserts SendText is denied when require_prompt_active=true + command_running. Test binary compile is currently blocked by pre-existing concurrent-agent breakage (E0603 macro select private, E0063 missing defer_fts_triggers field) unrelated to this fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-58uc0","title":"[gap] README wait-for rule-fire claim not matched by crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs","description":"README.md:144 claims `ft robot wait-for` blocks until a specific rule fires; grep shows the command is defined as "Wait for a pattern in pane output" at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:2454-2473 and implemented via `wezterm::compile_wait_matcher` + `PaneWaiter` polling at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:17782-17864.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:08.856774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.722606Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:14.644794Z","close_reason":"done — README said 'blocks until a specific rule fires' but implementation waits for pattern match in pane output; fixed wording (9f9a7c40)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
@@ -1272,7 +1274,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.15","title":"Release workflow audit: targets + reproducible flags + SHA256 + signing vs AGENTS.md Release Profile","description":"## Purpose

Audit `.github/workflows/release.yml` against wa-nu4.3.9 ship-readiness criteria:

1. Binaries built for x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, aarch64-apple-darwin, x86_64-apple-darwin (minimum per epic body).
2. Reproducible flags: `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH` + cargo release profile matching AGENTS.md §Release Profile (L136-147): opt-level="z", lto=true, codegen-units=1, panic="abort", strip=true.
3. SHA256 checksums published per asset + combined.
4. "Signed releases per AGENTS.md" — verify whether AGENTS.md actually mandates signing; if not, call that out; if so, cite the clause.

## Acceptance

- Close reason cites file:line evidence for each of the four checks.
- Any partial / missing coverage becomes a concrete follow-up bead with a concrete fix path.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:10.536449Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:18:20.142527Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:18:20.142177Z","close_reason":"AS-VERIFIED for 3/4 criteria; 2 surfaced gaps captured in follow-up beads.

1. TARGETS ✓ — release.yml:32-51 matrix:
   * x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu at L33 (required)
   * aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu at L37 (bonus)
   * x86_64-apple-darwin at L41 (required)
   * aarch64-apple-darwin at L45 (required)
   * x86_64-pc-windows-msvc at L49 (bonus)
   fail-fast:false at L29.

2. CARGO RELEASE PROFILE ✓ — Cargo.toml:547-552 matches AGENTS.md §Release Profile (L136-147) line-for-line: opt-level='z', lto=true, codegen-units=1, panic='abort', strip=true.

3. REPRODUCIBILITY PARTIAL — release.yml:65 sets SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH='0', but crates/frankenterm/build.rs:32-39 reads wall clock via 'date -u' and ignores the env var; FT_GIT_DIRTY at build.rs:17-29 depends on working-tree state. → Filed wa-nu4.3.9.16 (bug, P2) with concrete fix path.

4. SHA256 CHECKSUMS ✓ — per-asset: shasum -a 256 for unix (release.yml:89) + powershell Get-FileHash for windows (L98-99); combined SHA256SUMS manifest at L136.

5. SIGNING — AGENTS.md has zero mentions of sign/gpg/cosign/sigstore/provenance/attestation/slsa; §Release Profile does NOT mandate signing, so 'signed releases per AGENTS.md' is vacuously satisfied. release.yml has no signing either. Filed wa-nu4.3.9.17 (task, P3) as a ship-readiness follow-up with a concrete SLSA build provenance patch path (actions/attest-build-provenance@v2).

Conclusion: release.yml meets AGENTS.md §Release Profile DoD. Reproducibility has a real gap (w/ build.rs) that needs a code-scope agent; signing is a nice-to-have not in DoD.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","ci","distribution","release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.15","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:10.536449Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.16","title":"release build.rs: honor SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH for FT_BUILD_TS / FT_GIT_DIRTY so release.yml reproducibility env actually works","description":"## Bug

release.yml:65 sets `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH: "0"` to signal reproducible builds, but crates/frankenterm/build.rs:32-39 calls `date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` which reads wall-clock and ignores the env var. Same commit built on two different days produces different `FT_BUILD_TS` → different binary hashes → SHA256SUMS diverge for identical source state.

`FT_GIT_DIRTY` (build.rs:17-29) has a similar issue: depends on working-tree state, not commit content.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. `git checkout <tag>`
2. `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0 cargo build --release -p frankenterm`
3. Wait a second. Repeat.
4. `sha256sum target/release/ft` — hashes differ.

## Fix path

In crates/frankenterm/build.rs:

- If `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH` is set, format that epoch as UTC ISO8601 for `FT_BUILD_TS` instead of calling `date -u`.
- Either skip `git status --porcelain` entirely in reproducible mode, or gate `FT_GIT_DIRTY` on commit-level truth only.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Two back-to-back builds from the same commit with `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0` produce identical `ft` binaries on the same host/toolchain.
- `ft version --full` still shows a meaningful timestamp (the SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH-derived one).

## Testing

- Add a one-shot check script or CI step that builds twice with SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=0 and diffs the resulting binary hashes.

## Scope note

Requires editing crates/frankenterm/build.rs which is outside the current release-plumbing agent's scope — flagging for a code-scope agent. Surfaced by wa-nu4.3.9.15 release workflow audit (commit 83acd334).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:52.540553Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:24:54.064329Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:24:54.064081Z","close_reason":"build.rs now honors SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH (landed in 4d251588). When set: FT_BUILD_TS is derived from the epoch via GNU 'date -d @<e>' / BSD 'date -r <e>' with a 4-digit-year sanity check and epoch:<n> fallback; FT_GIT_DIRTY is forced empty regardless of working-tree state. Preserves the existing YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ format so main.rs consumers and tests are unaffected. Added cargo:rerun-if-env-changed=SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH. Verified: rustc --edition 2024 standalone parse=0, runtime sanity on macOS showed correct ISO8601 conversion for 0/1700000000/1735689600 and safe fallback for invalid input. Full cross-build verification (two builds produce identical binaries) is an acceptance item for a separate CI gate.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","release","reproducibility"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.16","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:52.540553Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.17","title":"release.yml: add SLSA build provenance attestation for published artifacts","description":"## Gap

AGENTS.md §Release Profile (L136-147) does NOT currently require signing, so strictly there's no DoD miss. But the wa-nu4.3.9 mission says 'A tool that controls terminals needs high trust.' Release artifacts currently ship with SHA256 checksums (release.yml:89/99/136) but no cryptographic provenance — a user can't verify an artifact was actually produced by this repo's workflow vs tampered in transit.

## Fix path (in-scope for workflow-only agent)

Add to `.github/workflows/release.yml` `release` job:

```yaml
permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write        # for sigstore OIDC token
  attestations: write    # for provenance upload
```

Add a step after download-artifact, before softprops/action-gh-release:

```yaml
- name: Generate SLSA build provenance attestations
  uses: actions/attest-build-provenance@v2
  with:
    subject-path: |
      dist/*.tar.xz
      dist/*.zip
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `gh attestation verify <artifact> --owner Dicklesworthstone` succeeds against a post-change release.
- No change to artifact naming, checksum publishing, or release notes.

## Testing

- `workflow_dispatch` the release workflow against a sacrificial tag; confirm attestations appear under the Releases page sigstore bundle and `gh attestation verify` passes.

## Why P3

Nice-to-have, not in AGENTS.md Release Profile DoD. Surfaced by wa-nu4.3.9.15 audit.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:18:02.794328Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:23:07.960564Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:23:07.960327Z","close_reason":"Landed 325054be to add GitHub artifact build provenance attestation in release.yml: release job now has id-token/attestations permissions and runs actions/attest@v4 over dist/*.tar.xz and dist/*.zip before publishing. Verified with YAML parse and git diff --check; live workflow_dispatch/gh attestation verify remains follow-up runtime proof.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distribution","release","security","supply-chain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.17","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:18:02.794328Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.18","title":"CI: version-smoke job asserts ft --version + ft version --full output shape (ubuntu + macos)","description":"## Gap

README Quickstart (bcc668a0) claims `ft --version` emits semver+short-git and `ft version --full` emits commit/built/rustc/target/features. Without a CI assertion on the actual binary output, a future build.rs change or clap wiring edit can silently break that claim — exactly the regression ft-b4mif surfaced.

release.yml:68-80 runs those commands at tag time but doesn't assert their output; ci.yml runs unit tests on build_meta functions but never exercises the real binary.

## Deliverable

New `version-smoke` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that:

1. Depends on `build` (reuses the release binaries it already produces).
2. Downloads the `ft-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu` and `ft-aarch64-apple-darwin` artifacts on matching runners.
3. Asserts `ft --version` output contains semver and a commit hash in parens.
4. Asserts `ft version --full` output contains semver header, `commit:` (7-40 hex or `unknown`), `built:` (ISO8601 / `epoch:<n>` / `unknown`), and `rustc:`/`target:`/`features:` fields.

Matrix over `ubuntu-latest` + `macos-14` to catch platform-conditional regressions.

## Acceptance

- New job visible in Actions tab under ci.yml.
- Runs in < 2min (no rebuild, only download + execute).
- No `continue-on-error`.
- Fails loudly with a specific error pointer when any assertion misses.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:29:34.012643Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:29:34.012643Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","docs","drift","release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.18","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:29:34.012643Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.18","title":"CI: version-smoke job asserts ft --version + ft version --full output shape (ubuntu + macos)","description":"## Gap

README Quickstart (bcc668a0) claims `ft --version` emits semver+short-git and `ft version --full` emits commit/built/rustc/target/features. Without a CI assertion on the actual binary output, a future build.rs change or clap wiring edit can silently break that claim — exactly the regression ft-b4mif surfaced.

release.yml:68-80 runs those commands at tag time but doesn't assert their output; ci.yml runs unit tests on build_meta functions but never exercises the real binary.

## Deliverable

New `version-smoke` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that:

1. Depends on `build` (reuses the release binaries it already produces).
2. Downloads the `ft-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu` and `ft-aarch64-apple-darwin` artifacts on matching runners.
3. Asserts `ft --version` output contains semver and a commit hash in parens.
4. Asserts `ft version --full` output contains semver header, `commit:` (7-40 hex or `unknown`), `built:` (ISO8601 / `epoch:<n>` / `unknown`), and `rustc:`/`target:`/`features:` fields.

Matrix over `ubuntu-latest` + `macos-14` to catch platform-conditional regressions.

## Acceptance

- New job visible in Actions tab under ci.yml.
- Runs in < 2min (no rebuild, only download + execute).
- No `continue-on-error`.
- Fails loudly with a specific error pointer when any assertion misses.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.2","title":"Docs: README/quickstart + architecture + CLI/robot/MCP reference","description":"# Task: Documentation for v0.1

## Goal
Make the project approachable for a new contributor and operable for users.

This bead is intentionally self-contained and mirrors PLAN Appendix A + F so contributors don’t need to dig through planning docs.

## Deliverables
### 1) README (operator-first)
- What wa is (WezTerm mux “terminal hypervisor” for agent fleets).
- Quickstart:
  - install wa
  - `wa setup`
  - `wa watch`
  - `wa status`
  - `wa query` / `wa events`
- Safety guarantees:
  - observe vs act split
  - no silent gaps
  - policy-gated sending + approvals

### 2) Architecture doc
- Ingest → Storage (SQLite+FTS5) → Pattern engine → Workflow engine → Robot/MCP.
- Deterministic state via OSC 133.
- Explicit GAP semantics.

### 3) CLI reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Human-oriented commands (rich output by default via `rich_rust`):
- `wa status` — panes + inferred agent state
- `wa watch` — run daemon (foreground/background)
- `wa stop` — stop watcher in workspace safely
- `wa events` — recent/unhandled events
- `wa query` — FTS search
- `wa send` — guarded send to a pane
- `wa reserve` — reserve a pane for exclusive use
- `wa reservations` — list active reservations
- `wa approve` — grant allow-once approvals
- `wa workflow` — run a workflow manually (guarded)
- `wa audit` — audit trail of actions (redacted)
- `wa history` — action timeline + undoability
- `wa undo` — undo supported actions (with confirmation)
- `wa rules` — list/test rule packs, show matching traces
- `wa accounts` — list/refresh accounts, show rotation picks
- `wa setup` — canonical WezTerm configuration (local/remote)
- `wa doctor` — environment checks (wezterm presence, DB health)
- `wa diag bundle` — sanitized diagnostic bundle export
- `wa export` — export slices/history/audit
- `wa web` — optional HTTP server (`fastapi_rust`) if enabled
- `wa tui` — optional TUI (`charmed_rust`) if enabled
- `wa sync` — optional sync (`asupersync`) if enabled

Robot mode commands (stable JSON envelope; token-efficient):
- `wa robot state [--domain <name>] [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot get-text <pane_id> [--tail N] [--escapes]`
- `wa robot send <pane_id> "<text>" [--no-newline] [--wait-for "<pat>"] [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot wait-for <pane_id> "<pat>" [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot search "<fts query>" [--pane-id <id>] [--since <iso8601>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot events [--unhandled] [--pane-id <id>] [--type <event>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id> [--force]`
- `wa robot reserve <pane_id> --ttl <secs> --reason <text>`
- `wa robot release <pane_id>`
- `wa robot reservations`
- `wa robot accounts [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot accounts refresh [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot rules list [--pack <name>]`
- `wa robot rules test "<text>" [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot quick-start`

### 4) MCP reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Tools (names are short and obvious):
- `wa.state`
- `wa.get_text`
- `wa.send`
- `wa.wait_for`
- `wa.search`
- `wa.events`
- `wa.workflow_run`
- `wa.accounts`
- `wa.accounts_refresh`
- `wa.rules_list`
- `wa.rules_test`
- `wa.reserve`
- `wa.release`
- `wa.reservations`

Resources:
- `wa://panes`
- `wa://events`
- `wa://accounts`
- `wa://workflows`
- `wa://rules`
- `wa://reservations`

### 5) Library integration map (PLAN Appendix F)
| Library | Role in `wa` |
|---------|--------------|
| `cass` (`/dp/coding_agent_session_search`) | Correlation + session archaeology; used in status + workflows |
| `caut` (`/dp/coding_agent_usage_tracker`) | Usage truth + selection; used in accounts + `handle_usage_limits` |
| `rich_rust` | Human-first CLI output (tables/panels/highlight) |
| `charmed_rust` | Optional interactive TUI (pane picker, event feed, transcript viewer) |
| `fastmcp_rust` | MCP tool surface for agent control (mirrors robot mode) |
| `fastapi_rust` | Optional HTTP server for dashboards/webhooks (read-only first) |
| `asupersync` | Remote bootstrap/sync layer (configs, binaries, DB snapshots) |
| `playwright` | Automate device auth flows with persistent profiles |
| `ast-grep` | Structure-aware codebase scans in “unstick agent” workflows |

## Testing / doc correctness
Docs must not drift.

- The quickstart commands shown in README should be validated regularly:
  - either by referencing existing E2E scripts, or
  - by adding a small “docs smoke” CI step that runs the exact commands from the docs in a controlled environment (tracked by `wa-nu4.3.9.9`).

- Robot mode docs should match the schema contract:
  - reference the versioned JSON schemas and keep field names consistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new engineer can get to a running watcher and see events with no oral tradition.
- CLI/robot/MCP references match implemented command surfaces and schemas.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.3","title":"Version/build metadata: `wa --version` shows semver + build info + enabled features","description":"# Task: Version/build metadata (`wa --version`)

## Goal
Make it easy to know exactly what build of wa is running when debugging bugs, comparing machines, or filing issues.

This becomes non-optional once we span multiple machines and (optionally) a vendored WezTerm build.

## Output contract
- `wa --version` prints a **single, stable** line by default (human-friendly, scriptable).
- `wa --version --verbose` prints a multi-line block with detailed metadata.
- Output must not require a TTY.

## Fields to include (when available)
- `wa` semver
- build timestamp (UTC)
- git commit hash (if built from a git checkout)
- rustc version
- target triple
- enabled cargo features (mcp/web/tui/sync/browser/vendored/etc.)

Vendored mode (PLAN Appendix H):
- when `--features vendored` is enabled, include:
  - vendored WezTerm commit hash (the pinned commit)
  - any compatibility/version string we can reliably extract

## Why this matters
When automation spans multiple machines, “what version is running?” is the first diagnostic question. Vendored mode adds an additional critical dimension (which WezTerm commit?).

## Testing
- Unit tests for `--version` output:
  - non-empty
  - contains semver
  - stable (no ANSI, no random ordering)

- Verbose output tests:
  - includes the detailed fields and feature list
  - when built with vendored feature (in CI matrix), includes vendored commit hash

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa --version` output is stable and does not require a TTY.
- `wa --version --verbose` provides enough metadata to uniquely identify the build.
- Vendored builds surface vendored commit metadata deterministically.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.4","title":"Release packaging: reproducible builds + single-binary install path","description":"# Task: Release packaging

## Goal
Provide a simple, reliable way for users to install and update wa:
- build a single lean binary
- ship artifacts with enough metadata to debug
- keep installation friction low

## Deliverables
- Release build produces a single binary (`cargo build --release`).
- Provide at least one installation path:
  - downloadable release artifact (preferred)
  - optional `cargo install --path .` for devs

- Document:
  - minimum supported OSes
  - required external deps (e.g., WezTerm installed)
  - feature flags and what they enable

## Reproducibility & safety
- Aim for reproducible builds (same inputs → same hash) where feasible.
- Publish checksums for release artifacts.
- Never require users to run unsafe install commands.

## Testing
- Smoke tests for the released binary:
  - `wa --version --verbose`
  - `wa --help`
  - `wa doctor` (in a minimal environment)

- Feature-matrix build verification in CI (tracked in CI bead):
  - default features
  - `--features mcp`
  - `--features web`
  - `--features tui`
  - `--features sync`
  - `--features browser`
  - when vendored exists: `--features vendored`

## Acceptance Criteria
- A user can install and run `wa --help` without cloning the repo (via a release artifact).
- Release artifacts include build metadata (`wa --version --verbose` is meaningful).
